other_observers: Norm Erthal, Allison Hilf, Joe Roller, Ira Sanders, Dick Schottler, Glenn Walbek; I'm the original finder of this bird.
specific_location: The trees along the inlet canal of Prewit Reservoir
description: Tanager by body size and body shape; very similar in size and shape to Western Tanager (Piranga ludoviciana). Tanager bill vice an orioles longer black, pointed bill. Back, neck and head all bright olive green; undersides (throat, breast, belly and undertail coverts) bright lemon yellow. Wings dark, not quite black and unmarked, no wingbar(s). Wings contrasted strongly with bright olive back and with the yellow underparts. Tail slightly paler than wings. Eyes black; bill pale.Behaviors: Feeding in the mid- and upper branches of Plains Cottonwoods and willow.Call: No vocalizations were heard.Plumage: fall
